author: codex
scope: relation endpoint-shape cleanup prep only; durable retire+replace mechanism planned but not applied

bucket.name: has_timeline_event metric-claim-to-event procurement schedule rows
bucket.why_coherent: After excluding retired submissions, prior relation cleanup batches017-036, and missing-target repair work, the next top exact endpoint-shape bucket has two meeting_doc_95301 has_timeline_event rows where a $8.2M procurement metric claim is incorrectly used as the timeline subject for committee and board meeting events. Metric claims are not durable timeline owners. Replacing them with supports event -> metric_claim preserves the source-backed connection between each meeting event and the procurement amount.

checkpoint_context.method: Read-only inventory over canonical JSONL with current endpoint-shape rules; excluded retired submissions, prior codex relation cleanup prep originals through batch036, and missing-target repair work. Did not run CLI validate, materialize, export-jsonl, DB repair/publish, or provider-backed commands.

endpoint_shape_probe.has_timeline_event metric_claim->event: Relation kind has_timeline_event expects project|route|corridor|entity|source|event -> event, got metric_claim -> event.

local_validation.tooling: createSubmissionEntry dry run for replacement tool_args plus relationEndpointIssues using known local ids metric_claim_meeting_doc_95301_8_2m_procurement, event_meeting_doc_95301_board_20220921, and event_meeting_doc_95301_committee_20220919; relationEndpointShapeIssue checked for supports event->metric_claim.

command_evidence.result: 1 before writing; artifact did not already exist.

command_evidence.command: endpoint-shape inventory script excluding retired submissions, cleanup batches017-036, and missing-target repair submissions
command_evidence.result: Remaining unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape total was 40; selected exact bucket has_timeline_event metric_claim->event had count 2.

command_evidence.command: canonical JSONL inspection for relation_meeting-doc-95301-procurement-board-meeting, relation_meeting-doc-95301-procurement-committee-meeting, metric_meeting-doc-95301-8-2m-procurement, event_meeting-doc-95301-board-20220921, and event_meeting-doc-95301-committee-20220919
command_evidence.result: Confirmed both originals are current canonical has_timeline_event metric_claim->event rows and confirmed endpoint local observation ids.

Confirmed p003_c0001 contains Board Action schedule dates, p003_c0009 contains the Schedule H $8.2M amount, and p002_c0003 states the procurement agenda includes one action for an estimated $8.2M expenditure.

command_evidence.command: createSubmissionEntry dry run for two supports event->metric_claim replacements
command_evidence.result: Both replacement entries accepted with no issues, no warnings, no relationEndpointIssues, and no endpoint-shape issue.

command_evidence.command: collision guard script over retired.json, data/submissions, and prior prep artifacts through batch036
command_evidence.result: No original retired/prior hits and no replacement submission id or local observation id collisions.

source_evidence.source_quote: Board Action Order To Date Approval Info Other 1 Committee 9/19/22 2 Board 9/21/22
Schedule H: Modifications to Personal/Miscellaneous Service Contracts 1 $ 8.2 M

retire.reason: The original relation uses a metric_claim as a has_timeline_event subject. The source supports a Board meeting event scheduled for 9/21/22 and a related $8.2M procurement amount, not a metric-owned timeline event.

tool_args.label: Board meeting supports $8.2M procurement metric
tool_args.raw_text: Board Action Order: Board 9/21/22; Schedule H procurement amount $8.2M.

payload.description: Replacement for malformed has_timeline_event metric_claim -> event relation; source schedules the procurement action for Board action on 9/21/22 and states a $8.2M Schedule H procurement amount.
